title: WSL: podman compose secrets does not work
body:
### Issue Description [podman compose](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-compose.1.html) does not mount [secrets](https://github.com/compose-spec/compose-spec/blob/main/09-secrets.md) correctly to the container. ### Steps to reproduce the issue 1. Example `compose.yaml` ```yaml services: postgres: image: postgres:16.4-bookworm ports: - 5432:5432 environment: POSTGRES_PASSWORD_FILE: /run/secrets/db-pass secrets: - db-pass secrets: db-pass: environment: TEST_PASSWORD ``` 2. Run the following CLI comand: ```sh TEST_PASSWORD=TestPass123 podman compose up ``` ### Describe the results you received ``` postgres-1 | /usr/local/bin/docker-entrypoint.sh: line 21: /run/secrets/db-pass: No such file or directory ``` ### Describe the results you expected container should start without any errors. ### podman info output

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Okay, those are the default subscription mounts. For a rootfull installation (Which I assume WSL also uses in the background) `touch /etc/containers/mounts.conf` is enough, for a rootless install it would be `$HOME/.config/containers/mounts.conf`

I have the same problem on MacOS. I think it might be related to permissions for writing into the `/run/secrets` folder (tmpfs mount). A workaround is to manually set the target location where the secret is to be placed, ie (in the `compose.yaml`):
```yaml
  myservice:
    secrets:
      - source: "mysecret"
        target: "/mysecret"
        mode: "444"
```
this places the secret `mysecret` in the root location `/`. Alternatively, one can manually add the `run/secrets` mount as `tmpfs` in the compose file, but this is not compatible to Docker (Compose) for more than one secret.

BTW https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/24084 is likely related to this issue.

> Okay, those are the default subscription mounts. For a rootfull installation (Which I assume WSL also uses in the background) `touch /etc/containers/mounts.conf` is enough, for a rootless install it would be `$HOME/.config/containers/mounts.conf`

@apollo13, thanks! This is a correct workaround.
For clarification - by default in the `podman-machine-default`, there is a file:
```
[root@computer ~]# cat /usr/share/containers/mounts.conf
/usr/share/rhel/secrets:/run/secrets
```
This is a default mount in Podman. Unfortunately the path `/run/secrets` is also used by compose by default, so there is a conflict.
Creating a file `touch /etc/containers/mounts.conf` disables default Podman's mount, allowing compose to use the default path.

@baude we have confirmation, that this is not Windows only issue. Changed title and label are incorrect.

Does anyone know what the default `/usr/share/rhel/secrets` mount is for? I don't see any info in the Podman docs.
